scheme: 6.  a survey, an experiment, or a public dataset

8.  e.g. modelling the arc of a basketball shot

scheme: 11.  A presentation, B communication, C engagement, D reflection, E use of maths
12.  C and D; add why you chose it / own data (C); add critical comments & limitations (D)
13.  description: 'the graph rises'; reflection: 'the model fits early data but fails later because growth cannot continue'
14.  introduction (aim/question) → maths (models, calculations, graphs) → reflection → conclusion
15.  it shows ongoing critical thinking, scoring better on criterion D
